decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to enhance lateral heat spreading away from the chip hot spot. I will apply a conservative 1.5x multiplier to the current spreader conductivity, moving it from 396.9 to 595.35 W/(m*K), which remains within the allowed upper bound of 500.0? No, 595 > 500. I must cap at the bound or use a smaller step. The bound is 500.0. Current is 396.9. Max allowed step is 2.0x (793.8), but hard bound is 500.0. So I will set it to the maximum allowable value of 500.0 to maximize cooling potential in this step.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity to the upper bound maximizes lateral heat diffusion, directly reducing the chip peak temperature violation.

expected_effects: Reduced thermal resistance in the spreader layer
Lower chip maximum temperature, potentially bringing it below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions
Setting to the absolute bound leaves no room for further increases in this variable in subsequent steps
